findings.description: Unit contains an explicit [GENERAL] governance directive requiring section designers to declare background color in component metadata so assemblers avoid duplicate adjacent backgrounds. No rule captures this compliance/governance obligation at all — it is entirely missing from the candidate set.

payload.description: Section designers must declare their section's background color in component metadata so assemblers can sequence sections without two identical adjacent backgrounds.

findings.description: The color-block delineation guidance (alternate White/Light Blue/Golden Sand/gradient backgrounds to distinguish topics) is a distinct design rule from the embedded [GENERAL] governance clause in the same unit. These two normative statements (design guidance vs. compliance mandate) should be split into separate rules rather than left unextracted or merged as one.

rules.description: Alternate White / Light Blue / Golden Sand / gradient section backgrounds so adjacent assembled sections don't visually merge (color blocks delineate topics).
Section designers must declare their section's background color in component metadata for assembler sequencing.

findings.description: The 'generous white space, never cluttered, limit focal points per section' guidance is a distinct layout rule not represented by any candidate rule for this blob.

payload.description: Use generous white space and limit focal points per section, using contrast to direct attention to the single most important element.

findings.description: The 'show, don't tell' guidance (break up text walls with imagery and icons) is a distinct content/layout rule not represented by any candidate rule for this blob.

payload.description: Show, don't tell: break up text walls with imagery and icons.

findings.description: Source explicitly calls the patient brochure the 'reference template for visuals, messaging, and overall tone of ALL LISRAYA materials' — this is a brand-wide mandatory reference, not a soft 'should' recommendation. Polarity/hardness understate the source's strength; should be must/hard_mandatory (or at least strong_default retained but polarity elevated to must).
